paper_map.title: Microstructure and tensile behavior of Fe-8Mn-6Al-0.2C low density steel
paper_map.abstract_summary: The microstructure and tensile behavior of Fe-8Mn-6Al-0.2C low density steel were investigated. After solution treatment at 900 °C for 1 h, the steel exhibits a duplex ferrite–austenite microstructure with ~30% austenite, achieving an ultimate tensile strength of 846 MPa and total elongation of 32%. The excellent mechanical properties are attributed to the TRIP effect, three-stage work hardening, and high dislocation density. The steel density is 6.95 g/cm³, about 11% lower than pure iron.
paper_map.research_objective: To investigate the transformation characteristics and mechanical stability of retained austenite, as well as the work hardening behavior and fracture mechanism, in Fe-8Mn-6Al-0.2C low-density steel.
main_process_variables: Solution treatment temperature (700–1000 °C, 1 h, water quenching)
Hot rolling (start 1150 °C, finish 850 °C, water quench to 550 °C, air cooling)
key_sections: Results - Properties and work hardening behavior
Results - Tensile behavior and austenite stability
